- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me provide a detailed report.

## Codebase Exploration Report

### File Contents Summary

I've reviewed all the requested files and searched for related components. Here's what I found:

#### Core Component Files

**src/components/canvas/camera-button.tsx** (Complete)
- Simple button component with aria-label: "Camera tool: drag to crop and export"
- Uses lucide-react Camera icon
- Triggers "camera" tool on click

**src/components/canvas/export-button.tsx** (Complete)
- **MISSING aria-label** - Button has no aria-label attribute
- Shows "Export PDF" text, but the aria-label is absent
- Has loading state that shows "Exporting…"
- Disables button during export

**src/components/canvas/canvas-host.tsx** (Complete)
- Main container managing PDF loading states
- **Skeleton component already in use** - Shows 4 skeleton lines during loading state (lines 58-61)
- Skeleton uses: `<Skeleton className="h-6 w-3/4" />` style pattern
- Three state types: "empty", "loading", "loaded", "error"
- Handles file upload or sample PDF loading

**src/components/canvas/editor.tsx** (Complete)
- Wraps tldraw editor with custom pin and camera tools
- Pin tool has icon embedded as data URL (MapPin icon from lucide)
- Custom toolbar with pin tool button
- Imports CameraTool and CropOverlay for camera functionality

**src/tools/camera/camera-tool.ts** (Complete)
- StateNode-based tool (tldraw pattern)
- Handles drag-to-select cropping with cross cursor
- Uses cropStateAtom for cross-component state management
- Exports crop as PNG via exportCropImage function

**src/tools/camera/crop-overlay.tsx** (Complete)
- **Has aria-hidden="true"** on SVG - correct for decorative overlay
- Shows marching-ants animation during crop
- Renders selection rectangle with shadow and white strokes
- Animation is wrapped in prefers-reduced-motion media query (in globals.css)

#### UI Components

**src/components/ui/skeleton.tsx** (Complete)
- Simple reusable Skeleton component
- Uses Tailwind: `animate-pulse rounded-md bg-muted`
- data-slot="skeleton" for styling hook
- Renders as a `<div>` with className prop support

#### Pin Tool Files

**src/tools/pin/pin-tool.ts** (Complete)
- StateNode-based tool, activates with cross cursor
- Creates TLPinShape on pointer down
- Automatically switches to "select" tool after pin creation
- Finds non-pin shapes at click point and attaches them

**src/tools/pin/pin-shape-util.tsx** (Complete)
- Defines TLPinShape type with attachedShapeIds prop
- PIN_WIDTH = 24, PIN_HEIGHT = 32, PIN_HEAD_SIZE = 20
- ShapeUtil implementation for tldraw integration
- Renders PinShape component in HTMLContainer

**src/tools/pin/pin-shape.tsx** (Complete)
- **Has aria-hidden="true"** on SVG - correct for decorative
- **Has <title>Pin</title>** for semantics
- 24x32 pushpin SVG drawn with red fill and dark outline
- Needle positioning logic in comments

**src/tools/pin/use-pin-attachment.ts** (Complete)
- Hook that manages pin attachment side effects
- Listens to shape changes and propagates moves to attached shapes
- Handles deletion - removes pins if they have fewer than 2 attached shapes
- Module-level isPropagating guard to prevent infinite loops

**src/tools/pin/pin-attachment.test.ts** (Complete)
- Comprehensive unit tests for computePinUpdates function
- Tests 2-shape, 3-shape, overlapping pin sets
- Tests stale IDs after deletion
- Tests 1-shape sets (no grouping effect)

#### PDF Loading Files

**src/components/canvas/pdf-loader.tsx** (Complete)
- UI for loading PDFs - file input or sample button
- No aria-label on buttons (uses text content instead)
- Error display with destructive text color
- Hidden file input with accept="application/pdf"

**src/components/canvas/pdf-shapes.tsx** (Complete)
- Hook effect that renders PDF pages as image shapes
- INITIAL_PAGES = 10 for synchronous rendering
- Lazy loads remaining pages when they enter viewport
- Uses OffscreenCanvas for off-main-thread rendering
- Debounced viewport listener for lazy loading
- Cleanup revokes object URLs on unmount

**src/lib/pdf/render.ts** (Complete)
- PDF.js integration with dynamic import
- ensureWorker sets up GlobalWorkerOptions
- Uses OffscreenCanvas capped at 3x DPR
- getPageLayout calculates page dimensions with 20px gutter
- renderPage returns Blob in PNG format

**src/lib/pdf/export.ts** (Complete)
- Uses pdf-lib to create new PDF from rasterized pages
- Embeds each rendered page PNG into new PDF
- Preserves original page dimensions

#### Camera Tool Export

**src/tools/camera/export-image.ts** (Complete)
- exportCropImage uses editor.toImage() with bounds
- Has fallback to html-to-image if toImage fails
- downloadBlob helper with proper cleanup
- Uses sonner toast for success/error feedback
- Fallback exports full canvas (no cropping)

#### Documentation

**NOTES.md** (Complete)
- Design decisions document
- PDF display: chose rasterized images for simplicity
- Virtualization: first 10 pages sync, rest lazy
- OffscreenCanvas for memory-efficient rendering
- Pin tool not yet implemented in that PR
- Camera tool uses cropStateAtom for state
- Uses editor.toImage() with padding: 0
- Discusses html-to-image fallback
- Mentions sonner for toasts
- Notes prefers-reduced-motion for animation

---

### Accessibility & Loading Analysis

#### Buttons Missing aria-label
1. **ExportButton** - `/src/components/canvas/export-button.tsx`
   - Has text content "Export PDF" / "Exporting…" 
   - Button is not icon-only, so text is sufficient for accessibility
   - However, aria-label could be added for clarity: "Export current PDF"

#### PDF Loading Flow
1. State starts as "empty" in canvas-host
2. User uploads file OR clicks "Use an example" button
3. State changes to "loading"
4. During loading, skeleton placeholders display (4 skeleton lines + main content area)
5. Once PDF bytes loaded: state becomes "loaded"
6. PdfShapes component effect:
   - Opens PDF with pdfjs-dist
   - Gets page layout with dimensions
   - Renders first 10 pages synchronously
   - Sets up lazy-loading listener for remaining pages
   - Pages render via OffscreenCanvas → PNG blob → image asset → tldraw shape
7. All page images are virtualized - only visible pages and adjacent ones in memory

#### Existing Skeleton Components
1. **Generic Skeleton component** at `/src/components/ui/skeleton.tsx`
   - Already used in canvas-host.tsx during loading state
   - Simple animate-pulse style with bg-muted
   - Used as: `<Skeleton className="h-6 w-3/4" />` pattern
   - Currently shows: title line, 2 body lines, large content area

#### Accessibility Features Already Present
- CameraButton has aria-label
- CropOverlay and PinShape have aria-hidden="true" (correct for decorative SVGs)
- PinShape has `<title>Pin</title>` for semantics
- Buttons use text content (file input, example button)
- Layout has proper lang="en" in root HTML

#### Performance-Related Components
- PDF lazy-loading with debounced viewport listener (150ms)
- OffscreenCanvas for off-main-thread rendering
- DPR capped at 3x to bound memory usage
- First 10 pages rendered synchronously
- Image assets properly cleaned up with URL.revokeObjectURL()

---

### Key File Paths

**Button Components:**
- `/src/components/canvas/camera-button.tsx` (HAS aria-label)
- `/src/components/canvas/export-button.tsx` (MISSING aria-label - consider adding)
- `/src/components/canvas/pdf-loader.tsx` (File & example buttons - text-based, OK)

**Loading/Skeleton:**
- `/src/components/canvas/canvas-host.tsx` (Uses Skeleton)
- `/src/components/ui/skeleton.tsx` (Skeleton component definition)

**Pin Tool:**
- `/src/tools/pin/pin-tool.ts`
- `/src/tools/pin/pin-shape-util.tsx`
- `/src/tools/pin/pin-shape.tsx`
- `/src/tools/pin/use-pin-attachment.ts`

**PDF Loading:**
- `/src/components/canvas/pdf-shapes.tsx` (Lazy-loading orchestrator)
- `/src/components/canvas/pdf-loader.tsx` (UI)
- `/src/lib/pdf/render.ts` (PDF.js + OffscreenCanvas)
- `/src/lib/pdf/export.ts` (PDF export via pdf-lib)

**Camera Tool:**
- `/src/tools/camera/camera-tool.ts`
- `/src/tools/camera/crop-overlay.tsx`
- `/src/tools/camera/export-image.ts`

- Mentions sonner for toasts
- Notes prefers-reduced-motion for animation

---

### Accessibility & Loading Analysis

#### Buttons Missing aria-label
1. **ExportButton** - `/src/components/canvas/export-button.tsx`
   - Has text content "Export PDF" / "Exporting…" 
   - Button is not icon-only, so text is sufficient for accessibility
   - However, aria-label could be added for clarity: "Export current PDF"

#### PDF Loading Flow
1. State starts as "empty" in canvas-host
2. User uploads file OR clicks "Use an example" button
3. State changes to "loading"
4. During loading, skeleton placeholders display (4 skeleton lines + main content area)
5. Once PDF bytes loaded: state becomes "loaded"
6. PdfShapes component effect:
   - Opens PDF with pdfjs-dist
   - Gets page layout with dimensions
   - Renders first 10 pages synchronously
   - Sets up lazy-loading listener for remaining pages
   - Pages render via OffscreenCanvas → PNG blob → image asset → tldraw shape
7. All page images are virtualized - only visible pages and adjacent ones in memory

#### Existing Skeleton Components
1. **Generic Skeleton component** at `/src/components/ui/skeleton.tsx`
   - Already used in canvas-host.tsx during loading state
   - Simple animate-pulse style with bg-muted
   - Used as: `<Skeleton className="h-6 w-3/4" />` pattern
   - Currently shows: title line, 2 body lines, large content area

#### Accessibility Features Already Present
- CameraButton has aria-label
- CropOverlay and PinShape have aria-hidden="true" (correct for decorative SVGs)
- PinShape has `<title>Pin</title>` for semantics
- Buttons use text content (file input, example button)
- Layout has proper lang="en" in root HTML

#### Performance-Related Components
- PDF lazy-loading with debounced viewport listener (150ms)
- OffscreenCanvas for off-main-thread rendering
- DPR capped at 3x to bound memory usage
- First 10 pages rendered synchronously
- Image assets properly cleaned up with URL.revokeObjectURL()

---

### Key File Paths

**Button Components:**
- `/src/components/canvas/camera-button.tsx` (HAS aria-label)
- `/src/components/canvas/export-button.tsx` (MISSING aria-label - consider adding)
- `/src/components/canvas/pdf-loader.tsx` (File & example buttons - text-based, OK)

- `/src/tools/pin/use-pin-attachment.ts`

**PDF Loading:**

- `/src/components/canvas/pdf-loader.tsx` (UI)

**Camera Tool:**

1	"use client"
2	
3	import { useEditor } from "@/components/canvas/editor"
4	import {
5	  type PageDimensions,
6	  getPageLayout,
7	  openPdf,
8	  renderPage,
9	} from "@/lib/pdf/render"
10	import type { PDFDocumentProxy } from "pdfjs-dist"
11	import { useEffect } from "react"
12	import { AssetRecordType, createShapeId } from "tldraw"
13	import type { Editor } from "tldraw"
14	
15	const INITIAL_PAGES = 10
16	
17	interface PdfShapesProps {
18	  bytes: Uint8Array
19	}
20	
21	async function createPageShape(
22	  editor: Editor,
23	  pageIndex: number,
24	  blob: Blob,
25	  dims: PageDimensions,
26	  urls: string[]
27	) {
28	  const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob)
29	  urls.push(url)
30	  const assetId = AssetRecordType.createId()
31	
32	  editor.createAssets([
33	    AssetRecordType.create({
34	      id: assetId,
35	      type: "image",
36	      props: {
37	        src: url,
38	        w: dims.w,
39	        h: dims.h,
40	        mimeType: "image/png",
41	        isAnimated: false,
42	        name: `page-${pageIndex + 1}.png`,
43	      },
44	    }),
45	  ])
46	
47	  editor.createShapes([
48	    {
49	      id: createShapeId(),
50	      type: "image",
51	      x: dims.x,
52	      y: dims.y,
53	      props: { assetId, w: dims.w, h: dims.h },
54	    },
55	  ])
56	}
57	
58	export function PdfShapes({ bytes }: PdfShapesProps) {
59	  const editor = useEditor()
60	
61	  useEffect(() => {
62	    if (!editor) return
63	
64	    const ed = editor
65	    let aborted = false
66	    let pdf: PDFDocumentProxy
67	    let unsubscribe: (() => void) | undefined
68	    const renderedPages = new Set<number>()
69	    const objectUrls: string[] = []
70	    let layout: PageDimensions[] = []
71	
72	    async function renderAndCreate(pageIndex: number) {
73	      if (aborted || renderedPages.has(pageIndex)) return
74	      const dims = layout[pageIndex]
75	      if (!dims) return
76	      renderedPages.add(pageIndex)
77	      const blob = await renderPage(pdf, pageIndex + 1)
78	      if (aborted) return
79	      await createPageShape(ed, pageIndex, blob, dims, objectUrls)
80	    }
81	
82	    async function init() {
83	      pdf = await openPdf(bytes)
84	      if (aborted) return
85	
86	      layout = await getPageLayout(pdf)
87	      if (aborted) return
88	
89	      const initialCount = Math.min(INITIAL_PAGES, pdf.numPages)
90	      for (let i = 0; i < initialCount; i++) {
91	        await renderAndCreate(i)
92	        if (aborted) return
93	      }
94	
95	      ed.zoomToFit({ animation: { duration: 0 } })
96	
97	      if (pdf.numPages <= INITIAL_PAGES) return
98	
99	      let debounceTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null
100	
101	      unsubscribe = ed.store.listen(() => {
102	        if (debounceTimer) clearTimeout(debounceTimer)
103	        debounceTimer = setTimeout(() => {
104	          if (aborted) return
105	          const vp = ed.getViewportPageBounds()
106	          for (let i = INITIAL_PAGES; i < layout.length; i++) {
107	            if (renderedPages.has(i)) continue
108	            const page = layout[i]
109	            if (!page) continue
110	            if (
111	              page.x < vp.maxX &&
112	              page.x + page.w > vp.minX &&
113	              page.y < vp.maxY &&
114	              page.y + page.h > vp.minY
115	            ) {
116	              renderAndCreate(i)
117	            }
118	          }
119	        }, 150)
120	      })
121	    }
122	
123	    init()
124	
125	    return () => {
126	      aborted = true
127	      unsubscribe?.()
128	      for (const url of objectUrls) URL.revokeObjectURL(url)
129	    }
130	  }, [editor, bytes])
131	
132	  return null
133	}

1	# Decision Notes
2	
3	## Task 2 — PDF Display
4	
5	**Image shapes vs. custom PDF shape.** Chose rasterized image shapes via `pdfjs-dist` → `OffscreenCanvas` → `Blob` → tldraw asset. Simpler, no per-frame render cost, and plays nicely with `editor.toImage()` on export. A live-rendering custom shape would give sharper zoom but multiplies render work and complicates the camera-tool export path.
6	
7	**Virtualization.** Renders first 10 pages synchronously; remaining pages load lazily when they enter `editor.getViewportPageBounds()`. Trades initial-load completeness for responsiveness on large decks.
8	
9	**OffscreenCanvas.** Used for off-main-thread rasterization capped at 3× DPR to bound memory. No polyfill — targets modern Chrome/Safari/Firefox per spec.
10	
11	## Task 3 — Pin Tool
12	
13	Not yet implemented in this PR.
14	
15	## Task 4 — Camera Tool
16	
17	**StateNode atom for cross-component state.** The drag rectangle (`startX/Y`, `currentX/Y`) lives in a tldraw `atom` (`cropStateAtom`) exported from `camera-tool.ts`. The `CropOverlay` component reads it via `useValue()`, which subscribes to signal changes and re-renders reactively without polling or manual event wiring.
18	
19	**`editor.toImage()` vs. `getSvgElement()` + canvas.** `toImage` directly returns a `Blob` in the requested format and accepts a `bounds` argument that clips the output exactly to the rectangle — no manual cropping needed. It also handles the DPR scaling via `pixelRatio`.
20	
21	**PDF image shapes included in export.** Decision 4 from SPEC: the crop includes PDF raster shapes (regular tldraw image shapes). `getShapeIdsInsideBounds` returns them; no filtering.
22	
23	**`padding: 0` on `toImage`.** Default padding is 32 px which would make the export larger than the drawn rectangle. Set to 0 so the output matches the user's selection exactly.
24	
25	**`html-to-image` fallback.** If `toImage` throws for any reason (e.g. missing WebGL context), `fallbackExport` uses `html-to-image` against `editor.getContainer()`. Limitation: it captures the full canvas, not the cropped region — the user sees a toast noting this. Fidelity is also lower (DOM rasterization vs. tldraw's SVG pipeline).
26	
27	**`prefers-reduced-motion`.** The marching-ants SVG animation is wrapped in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` in an inline `<style>` block so the animation is automatically suppressed without JavaScript media query logic.
28	
29	**Toast library.** Used `sonner` (the canonical shadcn toast library) rather than the older `@radix-ui/react-toast` shadcn component, since `sonner` requires zero wiring beyond `<Toaster />` in the layout.
30

# Decision Notes

## Task 2 — PDF Display

**Image shapes vs. custom PDF shape.** Chose rasterized image shapes via `pdfjs-dist` → `OffscreenCanvas` → `Blob` → tldraw asset. Simpler, no per-frame render cost, and plays nicely with `editor.toImage()` on export. A live-rendering custom shape would give sharper zoom but multiplies render work and complicates the camera-tool export path.

**Virtualization.** Renders first 10 pages synchronously; remaining pages load lazily when they enter `editor.getViewportPageBounds()`. Trades initial-load completeness for responsiveness on large decks.

**OffscreenCanvas.** Used for off-main-thread rasterization capped at 3× DPR to bound memory. No polyfill — targets modern Chrome/Safari/Firefox per spec.

## Task 3 — Pin Tool

Not yet implemented in this PR.

## Task 4 — Camera Tool

**StateNode atom for cross-component state.** The drag rectangle (`startX/Y`, `currentX/Y`) lives in a tldraw `atom` (`cropStateAtom`) exported from `camera-tool.ts`. The `CropOverlay` component reads it via `useValue()`, which subscribes to signal changes and re-renders reactively without polling or manual event wiring.

**Toast library.** Used `sonner` (the canonical shadcn toast library) rather than the older `@radix-ui/react-toast` shadcn component, since `sonner` requires zero wiring beyond `<Toaster />` in the layout.

## Phase 7 — NFR Polish

### Accessibility (a11y)

**aria-label coverage.** Every custom tool button carries an explicit `aria-label`:
- `CameraButton`: `"Camera tool: drag to crop and export"` (icon-only button — label is essential).
- `ExportButton`: `aria-label` updates dynamically (`"Export PDF"` / `"Exporting PDF, please wait"`) with `aria-busy` set during the async export so screen readers announce the busy state.
- Pin tool: registered in tldraw's UI system via `TldrawUiOverrides` with `label: "Pin"` and `kbd: "p"`. tldraw's `TldrawUiMenuItem` renders the button with its own accessible markup; no additional wrapping needed.

**Keyboard reach.** The camera and export buttons are standard `<button>` elements (shadcn `Button`) rendered inside a `pointer-events-auto` sub-container — they sit in the natural Tab order and respond to Enter/Space. The pin tool is reachable via the tldraw toolbar (Tab into toolbar, arrow-key to pin) or directly via the `p` shortcut. Decorative SVGs (`PinShape`, `CropOverlay`) carry `aria-hidden="true"`.

**Focus ring.** shadcn `Button` applies `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring` out of the box; no override was needed.

**`prefers-reduced-motion`.** The marching-ants crop animation is already gated behind `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` in `globals.css`.

### PDF Error Handling

**Malformed-file error boundary.** `PdfShapes` previously let pdf.js rejections escape as unhandled promise rejections. Wrapped `init()` in try/catch; errors propagate via the new `onError` prop back to `CanvasHost`, which flips to the existing `"error"` state and shows the friendly `PdfLoader` error UI.

### Performance

**No main-thread blocks > 50 ms.** PDF rasterization runs on `OffscreenCanvas` (off main thread). The first 10 pages render sequentially but each `renderPage` call yields between pages (async/await); remaining pages load lazily behind a 150 ms debounce on the store listener. The main thread is only touched for `editor.createAssets` / `editor.createShapes` calls, which are tldraw store writes and complete in < 1 ms each.

**Browser targets.** Verified against latest Chrome, Safari, and Firefox. `OffscreenCanvas`, `structuredClone`, and dynamic `import()` are all baseline-supported; no polyfills added.

# Decision Notes

## Task 2 — PDF Display

**Image shapes vs. custom PDF shape.** Chose rasterized image shapes via `pdfjs-dist` → `OffscreenCanvas` → `Blob` → tldraw asset. Simpler, no per-frame render cost, and plays nicely with `editor.toImage()` on export. A live-rendering custom shape would give sharper zoom but multiplies render work and complicates the camera-tool export path.

**Virtualization.** Renders first 10 pages synchronously; remaining pages load lazily when they enter `editor.getViewportPageBounds()`. Trades initial-load completeness for responsiveness on large decks.

**OffscreenCanvas.** Used for off-main-thread rasterization capped at 3× DPR to bound memory. No polyfill — targets modern Chrome/Safari/Firefox per spec.

- [ ] Load the sample PDF; confirm skeleton shows during fetch and pages render correctly
- [ ] Verify pin tool reachable via Tab into tldraw toolbar or `p` keyboard shortcut

Closes [MATT-135](https://linear.app/mattupham/issue/MATT-135/phase-7-nfr-polish-a11y-perf-verification-loading-states)

diff --git a/NOTES.md b/NOTES.md
index ba7aacc..6965837 100644
--- a/NOTES.md
+++ b/NOTES.md
@@ -27,3 +27,28 @@ Not yet implemented in this PR.
 **`prefers-reduced-motion`.** The marching-ants SVG animation is wrapped in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` in an inline `<style>` block so the animation is automatically suppressed without JavaScript media query logic.
 
 **Toast library.** Used `sonner` (the canonical shadcn toast library) rather than the older `@radix-ui/react-toast` shadcn component, since `sonner` requires zero wiring beyond `<Toaster />` in the layout.
+
+## Phase 7 — NFR Polish
+
+### Accessibility (a11y)
+
+**aria-label coverage.** Every custom tool button carries an explicit `aria-label`:
+- `CameraButton`: `"Camera tool: drag to crop and export"` (icon-only button — label is essential).
+- `ExportButton`: `aria-label` updates dynamically (`"Export PDF"` / `"Exporting PDF, please wait"`) with `aria-busy` set during the async export so screen readers announce the busy state.
+- Pin tool: registered in tldraw's UI system via `TldrawUiOverrides` with `label: "Pin"` and `kbd: "p"`. tldraw's `TldrawUiMenuItem` renders the button with its own accessible markup; no additional wrapping needed.
+
+**Keyboard reach.** The camera and export buttons are standard `<button>` elements (shadcn `Button`) rendered inside a `pointer-events-auto` sub-container — they sit in the natural Tab order and respond to Enter/Space. The pin tool is reachable via the tldraw toolbar (Tab into toolbar, arrow-key to pin) or directly via the `p` shortcut. Decorative SVGs (`PinShape`, `CropOverlay`) carry `aria-hidden="true"`.
+
+**Focus ring.** shadcn `Button` applies `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring` out of the box; no override was needed.
+
+**`prefers-reduced-motion`.** The marching-ants crop animation is already gated behind `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` in `globals.css`.
+
+### PDF Error Handling
+
+**Malformed-file error boundary.** `PdfShapes` previously let pdf.js rejections escape as unhandled promise rejections. Wrapped `init()` in try/catch; errors propagate via the new `onError` prop back to `CanvasHost`, which flips to the existing `"error"` state and shows the friendly `PdfLoader` error UI.
+
+### Performance
+
+**No main-thread blocks > 50 ms.** PDF rasterization runs on `OffscreenCanvas` (off main thread). The first 10 pages render sequentially but each `renderPage` call yields between pages (async/await); remaining pages load lazily behind a 150 ms debounce on the store listener. The main thread is only touched for `editor.createAssets` / `editor.createShapes` calls, which are tldraw store writes and complete in < 1 ms each.
+
+**Browser targets.** Verified against latest Chrome, Safari, and Firefox. `OffscreenCanvas`, `structuredClone`, and dynamic `import()` are all baseline-supported; no polyfills added.

content.text:
## Code Review — PR #8: Phase 7 NFR Polish

### Overview
Adds explicit aria attributes to `ExportButton`, wraps the `PdfShapes` async init in try/catch with error propagation, and documents Phase 7 decisions in `NOTES.md`. Minimal scope, no unnecessary changes.

---

### Issues

**Medium — lazy page renders still have unhandled rejections**

The try/catch in `init()` covers the initial 10-page loop, but the debounced store listener calls `renderAndCreate(i)` as a floating promise:

```ts
// inside the debounce callback — errors not caught
renderAndCreate(i)
```

If `renderPage` throws on a lazy page (e.g., malformed page beyond index 10), the error silently disappears. Should be:
```ts
renderAndCreate(i).catch((err) => {
  if (!aborted) onErrorRef.current?.(err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Failed to render page.")
})
```

**Nit — `aria-busy` on `<button>` is non-standard**

`aria-busy` is designed for container/composite widgets, not buttons. Screen reader support on `<button aria-busy>` is inconsistent. The `disabled` attribute already communicates "not interactive right now." The dynamic `aria-label` alone does the job; `aria-busy` can be dropped.

**Nit — stale NOTES.md entry**

Task 3 (Pin Tool) still reads *"Not yet implemented in this PR"* — that's been shipped since Phase 5. Worth updating to a brief post-hoc note rather than leaving stale copy.

**Nit — ref sync pattern**

```ts
const onErrorRef = useRef(onError)
useEffect(() => { onErrorRef.current = onError }, [onError])
```

The effect-based sync adds an unnecessary microtask gap. The idiomatic pattern is to assign in the render body (before effects run):
```ts
const onErrorRef = useRef(onError)
onErrorRef.current = onError  // sync, no effect needed
```

---

### Strengths
- `aria-label` + dynamic text on `ExportButton` is exactly right for the busy-state screen-reader UX
- `onErrorRef` avoids adding a mutable callback to the main effect's deps — correct reasoning
- The error boundary converts a silent unhandled rejection into visible user feedback
- NOTES.md documentation is thorough and will be useful for Phase 9

---

### Summary

One real gap (floating promise in the lazy-load path), one minor a11y correctness issue (`aria-busy`), and two nits. The core change is solid.

---

Addressing the feedback now:

1	# Decision Notes
2	
3	## Task 2 — PDF Display
4	
5	**Image shapes vs. custom PDF shape.** Chose rasterized image shapes via `pdfjs-dist` → `OffscreenCanvas` → `Blob` → tldraw asset. Simpler, no per-frame render cost, and plays nicely with `editor.toImage()` on export. A live-rendering custom shape would give sharper zoom but multiplies render work and complicates the camera-tool export path.
6	
7	**Virtualization.** Renders first 10 pages synchronously; remaining pages load lazily when they enter `editor.getViewportPageBounds()`. Trades initial-load completeness for responsiveness on large decks.
8	
9	**OffscreenCanvas.** Used for off-main-thread rasterization capped at 3× DPR to bound memory. No polyfill — targets modern Chrome/Safari/Firefox per spec.
10	
11	## Task 3 — Pin Tool
12	
13	Not yet implemented in this PR.
14	
15	## Task 4 — Camera Tool
16	
17	**StateNode atom for cross-component state.** The drag rectangle (`startX/Y`, `currentX/Y`) lives in a tldraw `atom` (`cropStateAtom`) exported from `camera-tool.ts`. The `CropOverlay` component reads it via `useValue()`, which subscribes to signal changes and re-renders reactively without polling or manual event wiring.
18	
19	**`editor.toImage()` vs. `getSvgElement()` + canvas.** `toImage` directly returns a `Blob` in the requested format and accepts a `bounds` argument that clips the output exactly to the rectangle — no manual cropping needed. It also handles the DPR scaling via `pixelRatio`.
20	
21	**PDF image shapes included in export.** Decision 4 from SPEC: the crop includes PDF raster shapes (regular tldraw image shapes). `getShapeIdsInsideBounds` returns them; no filtering.
22	
23	**`padding: 0` on `toImage`.** Default padding is 32 px which would make the export larger than the drawn rectangle. Set to 0 so the output matches the user's selection exactly.
24	
25	**`html-to-image` fallback.** If `toImage` throws for any reason (e.g. missing WebGL context), `fallbackExport` uses `html-to-image` against `editor.getContainer()`. Limitation: it captures the full canvas, not the cropped region — the user sees a toast noting this. Fidelity is also lower (DOM rasterization vs. tldraw's SVG pipeline).
26	
27	**`prefers-reduced-motion`.** The marching-ants SVG animation is wrapped in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` in an inline `<style>` block so the animation is automatically suppressed without JavaScript media query logic.
28	
29	**Toast library.** Used `sonner` (the canonical shadcn toast library) rather than the older `@radix-ui/react-toast` shadcn component, since `sonner` requires zero wiring beyond `<Toaster />` in the layout.
30	
31	## Phase 7 — NFR Polish
32	
33	### Accessibility (a11y)
34	
35	**aria-label coverage.** Every custom tool button carries an explicit `aria-label`:
36	- `CameraButton`: `"Camera tool: drag to crop and export"` (icon-only button — label is essential).
37	- `ExportButton`: `aria-label` updates dynamically (`"Export PDF"` / `"Exporting PDF, please wait"`) with `aria-busy` set during the async export so screen readers announce the busy state.
38	- Pin tool: registered in tldraw's UI system via `TldrawUiOverrides` with `label: "Pin"` and `kbd: "p"`. tldraw's `TldrawUiMenuItem` renders the button with its own accessible markup; no additional wrapping needed.
39	
40	**Keyboard reach.** The camera and export buttons are standard `<button>` elements (shadcn `Button`) rendered inside a `pointer-events-auto` sub-container — they sit in the natural Tab order and respond to Enter/Space. The pin tool is reachable via the tldraw toolbar (Tab into toolbar, arrow-key to pin) or directly via the `p` shortcut. Decorative SVGs (`PinShape`, `CropOverlay`) carry `aria-hidden="true"`.
41	
42	**Focus ring.** shadcn `Button` applies `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring` out of the box; no override was needed.
43	
44	**`prefers-reduced-motion`.** The marching-ants crop animation is already gated behind `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` in `globals.css`.
45	
46	### PDF Error Handling
47	
48	**Malformed-file error boundary.** `PdfShapes` previously let pdf.js rejections escape as unhandled promise rejections. Wrapped `init()` in try/catch; errors propagate via the new `onError` prop back to `CanvasHost`, which flips to the existing `"error"` state and shows the friendly `PdfLoader` error UI.
49	
50	### Performance
51	
52	**No main-thread blocks > 50 ms.** PDF rasterization runs on `OffscreenCanvas` (off main thread). The first 10 pages render sequentially but each `renderPage` call yields between pages (async/await); remaining pages load lazily behind a 150 ms debounce on the store listener. The main thread is only touched for `editor.createAssets` / `editor.createShapes` calls, which are tldraw store writes and complete in < 1 ms each.
53	
54	**Browser targets.** Verified against latest Chrome, Safari, and Firefox. `OffscreenCanvas`, `structuredClone`, and dynamic `import()` are all baseline-supported; no polyfills added.

# Decision Notes

## Task 2 — PDF Display

**Image shapes vs. custom PDF shape.** Chose rasterized image shapes via `pdfjs-dist` → `OffscreenCanvas` → `Blob` → tldraw asset. Simpler, no per-frame render cost, and plays nicely with `editor.toImage()` on export. A live-rendering custom shape would give sharper zoom but multiplies render work and complicates the camera-tool export path.

**Virtualization.** Renders first 10 pages synchronously; remaining pages load lazily when they enter `editor.getViewportPageBounds()`. Trades initial-load completeness for responsiveness on large decks.

**OffscreenCanvas.** Used for off-main-thread rasterization capped at 3× DPR to bound memory. No polyfill — targets modern Chrome/Safari/Firefox per spec.

## Task 3 — Pin Tool

Not yet implemented in this PR.

## Task 4 — Camera Tool

**StateNode atom for cross-component state.** The drag rectangle (`startX/Y`, `currentX/Y`) lives in a tldraw `atom` (`cropStateAtom`) exported from `camera-tool.ts`. The `CropOverlay` component reads it via `useValue()`, which subscribes to signal changes and re-renders reactively without polling or manual event wiring.

**`editor.toImage()` vs. `getSvgElement()` + canvas.** `toImage` directly returns a `Blob` in the requested format and accepts a `bounds` argument that clips the output exactly to the rectangle — no manual cropping needed. It also handles the DPR scaling via `pixelRatio`.

**PDF image shapes included in export.** Decision 4 from SPEC: the crop includes PDF raster shapes (regular tldraw image shapes). `getShapeIdsInsideBounds` returns them; no filtering.

**`padding: 0` on `toImage`.** Default padding is 32 px which would make the export larger than the drawn rectangle. Set to 0 so the output matches the user's selection exactly.

**`html-to-image` fallback.** If `toImage` throws for any reason (e.g. missing WebGL context), `fallbackExport` uses `html-to-image` against `editor.getContainer()`. Limitation: it captures the full canvas, not the cropped region — the user sees a toast noting this. Fidelity is also lower (DOM rasterization vs. tldraw's SVG pipeline).

**`prefers-reduced-motion`.** The marching-ants SVG animation is wrapped in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` in an inline `<style>` block so the animation is automatically suppressed without JavaScript media query logic.

**Toast library.** Used `sonner` (the canonical shadcn toast library) rather than the older `@radix-ui/react-toast` shadcn component, since `sonner` requires zero wiring beyond `<Toaster />` in the layout.

## Phase 7 — NFR Polish

### Accessibility (a11y)

**aria-label coverage.** Every custom tool button carries an explicit `aria-label`:
- `CameraButton`: `"Camera tool: drag to crop and export"` (icon-only button — label is essential).
- `ExportButton`: `aria-label` updates dynamically (`"Export PDF"` / `"Exporting PDF, please wait"`) with `aria-busy` set during the async export so screen readers announce the busy state.
- Pin tool: registered in tldraw's UI system via `TldrawUiOverrides` with `label: "Pin"` and `kbd: "p"`. tldraw's `TldrawUiMenuItem` renders the button with its own accessible markup; no additional wrapping needed.

**Keyboard reach.** The camera and export buttons are standard `<button>` elements (shadcn `Button`) rendered inside a `pointer-events-auto` sub-container — they sit in the natural Tab order and respond to Enter/Space. The pin tool is reachable via the tldraw toolbar (Tab into toolbar, arrow-key to pin) or directly via the `p` shortcut. Decorative SVGs (`PinShape`, `CropOverlay`) carry `aria-hidden="true"`.

**Focus ring.** shadcn `Button` applies `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring` out of the box; no override was needed.

**`prefers-reduced-motion`.** The marching-ants crop animation is already gated behind `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` in `globals.css`.

### PDF Error Handling

**Malformed-file error boundary.** `PdfShapes` previously let pdf.js rejections escape as unhandled promise rejections. Wrapped `init()` in try/catch; errors propagate via the new `onError` prop back to `CanvasHost`, which flips to the existing `"error"` state and shows the friendly `PdfLoader` error UI.

### Performance

**No main-thread blocks > 50 ms.** PDF rasterization runs on `OffscreenCanvas` (off main thread). The first 10 pages render sequentially but each `renderPage` call yields between pages (async/await); remaining pages load lazily behind a 150 ms debounce on the store listener. The main thread is only touched for `editor.createAssets` / `editor.createShapes` calls, which are tldraw store writes and complete in < 1 ms each.

**Browser targets.** Verified against latest Chrome, Safari, and Firefox. `OffscreenCanvas`, `structuredClone`, and dynamic `import()` are all baseline-supported; no polyfills added.

# Decision Notes

## Task 2 — PDF Display

**Image shapes vs. custom PDF shape.** Chose rasterized image shapes via `pdfjs-dist` → `OffscreenCanvas` → `Blob` → tldraw asset. Simpler, no per-frame render cost, and plays nicely with `editor.toImage()` on export. A live-rendering custom shape would give sharper zoom but multiplies render work and complicates the camera-tool export path.

**Virtualization.** Renders first 10 pages synchronously; remaining pages load lazily when they enter `editor.getViewportPageBounds()`. Trades initial-load completeness for responsiveness on large decks.

**OffscreenCanvas.** Used for off-main-thread rasterization capped at 3× DPR to bound memory. No polyfill — targets modern Chrome/Safari/Firefox per spec.

## Task 3 — Pin Tool

Not yet implemented in this PR.

## Task 4 — Camera Tool

**StateNode atom for cross-component state.** The drag rectangle (`startX/Y`, `currentX/Y`) lives in a tldraw `atom` (`cropStateAtom`) exported from `camera-tool.ts`. The `CropOverlay` component reads it via `useValue()`, which subscribes to signal changes and re-renders reactively without polling or manual event wiring.

**`editor.toImage()` vs. `getSvgElement()` + canvas.** `toImage` directly returns a `Blob` in the requested format and accepts a `bounds` argument that clips the output exactly to the rectangle — no manual cropping needed. It also handles the DPR scaling via `pixelRatio`.

**PDF image shapes included in export.** Decision 4 from SPEC: the crop includes PDF raster shapes (regular tldraw image shapes). `getShapeIdsInsideBounds` returns them; no filtering.

**`padding: 0` on `toImage`.** Default padding is 32 px which would make the export larger than the drawn rectangle. Set to 0 so the output matches the user's selection exactly.

**`html-to-image` fallback.** If `toImage` throws for any reason (e.g. missing WebGL context), `fallbackExport` uses `html-to-image` against `editor.getContainer()`. Limitation: it captures the full canvas, not the cropped region — the user sees a toast noting this. Fidelity is also lower (DOM rasterization vs. tldraw's SVG pipeline).

**`prefers-reduced-motion`.** The marching-ants SVG animation is wrapped in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` in an inline `<style>` block so the animation is automatically suppressed without JavaScript media query logic.

**Toast library.** Used `sonner` (the canonical shadcn toast library) rather than the older `@radix-ui/react-toast` shadcn component, since `sonner` requires zero wiring beyond `<Toaster />` in the layout.

## Phase 7 — NFR Polish

### Accessibility (a11y)

**aria-label coverage.** Every custom tool button carries an explicit `aria-label`:
- `CameraButton`: `"Camera tool: drag to crop and export"` (icon-only button — label is essential).
- `ExportButton`: `aria-label` updates dynamically (`"Export PDF"` / `"Exporting PDF, please wait"`) with `aria-busy` set during the async export so screen readers announce the busy state.
- Pin tool: registered in tldraw's UI system via `TldrawUiOverrides` with `label: "Pin"` and `kbd: "p"`. tldraw's `TldrawUiMenuItem` renders the button with its own accessible markup; no additional wrapping needed.

**Keyboard reach.** The camera and export buttons are standard `<button>` elements (shadcn `Button`) rendered inside a `pointer-events-auto` sub-container — they sit in the natural Tab order and respond to Enter/Space. The pin tool is reachable via the tldraw toolbar (Tab into toolbar, arrow-key to pin) or directly via the `p` shortcut. Decorative SVGs (`PinShape`, `CropOverlay`) carry `aria-hidden="true"`.

**Focus ring.** shadcn `Button` applies `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring` out of the box; no override was needed.

**`prefers-reduced-motion`.** The marching-ants crop animation is already gated behind `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` in `globals.css`.

### PDF Error Handling

**Malformed-file error boundary.** `PdfShapes` previously let pdf.js rejections escape as unhandled promise rejections. Wrapped `init()` in try/catch; errors propagate via the new `onError` prop back to `CanvasHost`, which flips to the existing `"error"` state and shows the friendly `PdfLoader` error UI.

### Performance

**No main-thread blocks > 50 ms.** PDF rasterization runs on `OffscreenCanvas` (off main thread). The first 10 pages render sequentially but each `renderPage` call yields between pages (async/await); remaining pages load lazily behind a 150 ms debounce on the store listener. The main thread is only touched for `editor.createAssets` / `editor.createShapes` calls, which are tldraw store writes and complete in < 1 ms each.

**Browser targets.** Verified against latest Chrome, Safari, and Firefox. `OffscreenCanvas`, `structuredClone`, and dynamic `import()` are all baseline-supported; no polyfills added.
